mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings. This mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in > 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 9.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as equity valuations compress on earnings recession expectations.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 9.5%, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, this indicates demand shock that propagates to corporate earnings within 60 trading days, producing negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352073M and HOUST at 529K suggest moderate demand
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% MoM, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) below 99.5 indicates supply-side weakness and capacity underutilization. This signals reduced corporate profitability and pricing power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 99.5, 5-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 52% of instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) move inversely. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 50K monthly while UNRATE remains elevated above 9%, this signals labor market stalling that constrains consumer income and produces negative 20-day equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130996K and UNRATE at 10.0% indicate labor market slack
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE level is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 217 combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 0.5% creates negative real rates that compress equity risk premiums. This regime-specific mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedge.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 216.385 near threshold, FEDFUNDS at 0.12%
regime_hint: negative real rates with elevated inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth trajectory measured over 5-quarter rolling windows predicts equity returns. When GDPC1 growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, this signals late-cycle slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 12990.3B indicates mature expansion phase
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly measure productive capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines while PAYEMS growth remains positive, this signals productivity deterioration and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM while PAYEMS growth > 0
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines MoM and PAYEMS growth > 0, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
